prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
1. *"Lo! In this third quarter of the Year of Our Lord Two Thousand and Twenty-Four, the alchemists of ‘Salesforce’ didst conjure great riches from the aether, their ‘cloud’ yielding a bounty of 9.13 billion golden talents—though none could say whence these coins materialized, save that they were ‘recurring’ like the tides or the plague."*

2. *"The sorcerers of ‘Churn’ didst whisper dark tidings, for many a peasant—nay, ‘customer’—did abandon their subscription scrolls, fleeing as though pursued by the Black Death, yet the high priests of ‘Net Revenue Retention’ did wave their hands and declare it a ‘healthy 112%,’ a mystery as profound as the Holy Trinity."*

3. *"Behold! The ‘AI-driven features’ didst multiply like loaves and fishes, though none could explain their purpose, save that they ‘enhanced workflows’—a phrase as empty as a monk’s stomach after Lent, yet the investors did cheer as though witnessing the Second Coming (or at least a modest uptick in ‘monthly active users’)."*

4. *"And thus did the CEO, a man of strange robes (or perhaps a ‘patagonia vest’), ascend the dais to proclaim, ‘We are laser-focused on scaling our hypergrowth synergies,’ words which the scribe doth record with trembling hand, for surely this is the tongue of angels—or of demons, or perhaps just of men who have drunk too much mead at the quarterly offsite."*
